intro x
    calc
      (fun x => f.symm (g.symm x)) (fun x => g (f x)) x = (fun x => f.symm (g.symm x)) (g (f x)) := rfl
      _ = f.symm (g.symm (g (f x))) := rfl

      _ = x := by

have h_main : ∀ (x : R), f.symm (g.symm (g (f x))) = x := by
      intro x
      have h1 : g.symm (g (f x)) = f x := by
        -- Since `g` is an isomorphism, `g.symm` is its left inverse.
        have h1 : Function.LeftInverse g.symm g := g.left_inv
        -- Apply the left inverse property to `f x : S`.
        have h2 : g.symm (g (f x)) = f x := h1 (f x)
        exact h2
      -- Since `f` is an isomorphism, `f.symm` is its left inverse.
      have h2 : Function.LeftInverse f.symm f := f.left_inv
      -- Simplify the expression using `h1`.
      calc
        f.symm (g.symm (g (f x))) = f.symm (f x) := by rw [h1]
        _ = x := h2 x
    -- Use the main lemma to conclude the proof.
    intro x
    have h := h_main x
    exact h
